judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports fully and correctly hit all three expectations with nearly identical technical substance; Report 2 adds extra corroboration (git history, secondary auth-gap note) but neither is meaningfully better on the graded expectations.

report_1.evidence: It doesn't take effect because the app connects as cedar_owner — the same role that owns the tables... So cedar_owner is exempt from the tenant_isolation policy on both tables, regardless of what app.current_tenant is set to.

report_2.evidence: Per Postgres semantics, ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY without FORCE exempts the table's owner from the policy... Since the app connects as cedar_owner, the table owner, every query the app runs is silently exempt from the tenant_isolation policy, regardless of what current_setting('app.current_tenant') is set to.

report_1.evidence: Change the application's actual runtime DATABASE_URL (cedarline/.env, and the checked-in cedarline/.env.example template...) to authenticate as cedar_app — the already-defined least-privilege role... Reserve cedar_owner exclusively for schema changes: it should only ever be the identity used by scripts/migrate.sh..., never by the running FastAPI process

report_2.evidence: Immediate fix: point the application's runtime DATABASE_URL at cedar_app, not cedar_owner... Structural change: stop sharing one DATABASE_URL between 'migrations/DDL' (legitimately needs owner privileges) and 'the running application' (should always be least-privilege). Introduce two distinct connection strings

report_1.evidence: The only file that creates cedar_app is cedarline/db/bootstrap/create_app_role.sql, and its own header comment states it explicitly: "One-time bootstrap... Not part of automatic database initialization; run by hand on first setup." Neither scripts/reset-local-db.sh nor scripts/migrate.sh invokes it. So after any wipe+rebuild, cedar_app simply does not exist in the new cluster.

report_2.evidence: The only place cedar_app is created anywhere in this repo is cedarline/db/bootstrap/create_app_role.sql, whose own header comment says: "One-time bootstrap: dedicated application role. Not part of automatic database initialization; run by hand on first setup."... reset-local-db.sh was added later... and never calls it.
